prompt: The replace() call does not remove all dots. What is the bug?
choices: replaceAll() is not available on String
In a regex, '.' matches any character, not just a literal dot; every character is replaced with '-'
replace() should be used instead of replaceAll() for single characters
The replacement string '-' must be escaped as '\\-'
explanation: replaceAll() interprets the first argument as a regex. In regex, '.' is a wildcard matching any character, so every character gets replaced with '-' → '-----'. Fix: escape the dot: replaceAll("\\.", "-") or use replace(".", "-") which takes a literal string.
